channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: When are PC parts going to be affordable again? — 27 replies — 68.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
When are PC parts going to be affordable again?

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-05-31 13:02 UTC

OP

When are PC parts going to be affordable again? I'm tired of not being able to play games made in this decade.

Early replies (first 3)

62294333
>>62294325
Stop playing games you child

62294453
>>62294325
Not sure but Macbook Neo seems reasonably priced to me.

62294457
>>62294333
This. You should be focusing on activities that will lift you up from not being able to afford PC parts.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62294531
>>62294325
>playing modern woke slop

62294535
>>62294333
tsmt. My desktop has been gathering dust for the past 7-8 years. I just use an old laptop for work and music and if I want to play a game I get my 360 or ps2.

62294539
>>62294325
if you invested in companies that made ur pc parts expensive you should have 5x the money to buy ur pc parts

62294553
>>62294325
a fully prebuilt 5090 pc is like 6k still, if you could not afford it before, you still cant afford it now
nothing has changed, problem is you are being too poor
if you had more money like anons said this would not even be a problem

keep in mind you would keep this pc for the next 3 years or until the connector burns up because nv release cycles are now longer so 2k a year at worst, if you wanna stay on the bleeding edge, you cant afford 2k a year on pc? get a job nikker

>>62294457
>>62294531
>>62294539
truez

62294556
>>62294325
I’m playing the game with all the computer parts for the masses

62294586
>>62294539
>If you bought the right lottery ticket you'd be rich

62294598
>>62294586
ram prices started to increase exponentially back in October, you literally had 4 months to see this coming

62294638
>>62294598
you had 10 years to predict the AI boom

62294654
>>62294638
I believed in my ram predictions, did you believe in your ai predictions

62294665
>>62294654
I invested in amd and nvidia in 2019 so, yeah.

62294669
>>62294325
If you haven't bought spare gaming pcs after seeing what happened to NIVIDIA GPUs, it's honestly on you.
I saw the writing in the wall and bought a cheap gaming pc with an AMD GPU for $700 on black friday and just held on to it just in case my current one breaks so I probably have 10-20 years before having to get a new one.

62294681
>>62294665
ok so we have the same opinion that this retard should have invested?

62294719
>Might have to save up for an extra another couple of months for gayming machine
Aww diddums

62294724
How old are you people?
Is this board filled with teens?
fuck your video games, grow up you useless fat incel faggot

62294742
>>62294453

I was watching a raid array rebuild and zening out and it hit me, normies like you, apple marketing shill spammers, you will never be able to have an offline LAN

You are too stupid and lazy. It's simply beyond you.The reasons why you would need one are incomprehensibe to someone who thinks brands are status symbols. The fact that LAN cables don't have a brand that signifies status to fools means you can't even understand why LAN cables exist or who would want one. I mean you know you are lying and selling awful underpowered overpriced 8gb uninspired thrash that traps consumers into an awful corporate walled garden cloud portal devices hell but you are so lacking ability, characater, morals, technical ability you are willing to spam this junk for low wages. That's your life. You probably spend most of it on food and rent and then use the free apple homosexual mating call product you get to sit in starbucks trying to hit on other men, hoping some elderly faggot will adopt you and you can live with them as a carer/prositute in exchange for some near geriatric ejaculating in your anus. Even if you won a lottery you would spend it all on overpriced, poor value brandslop logos and subscriptions, the little brain you have was completely overpowered by modern marketing, so much so you ended up a minion of it.

You're the perfect example of the future digital slave, you will have to subscribe to get access to remote storage, cloud games, music, movies and computing resources accessed via terrible tiny screened, overpriced devices with awful specs deliberately designed to be useless for anything other that cloud access portals. Yet you will proudly display your slave brand logo as if telling the world you are a good slave before you go and let some geriatric ejaculate in your anus for food and then spend any time you have left spamming apple marketing idiocy in desperation to get another free sample because it means everything to you to own gank brandslop

62294743
>>62294333
I refuse there people over the age of 21 who still play video games who aren't influencers paid to do it

62294746
>>62294681
yeah, only normalfags didn't find it obvious
we had pimeyes in 2019 and gpt in 2020

62294758
>>62294742
Least schizophrenic LAN shill

62294762
>>62294743
>>62294724
>I'm a big boy, I don't need toys anymore
You're stuck at the mental age of a 14 year old. Vidya is for truly mature people.

62294794
>>62294325
They won't be until nearly 2028 at earliest but the problem then will be that a lot of the companies that support pc building like case maers, fan, makers, psu makers, thermal paste makers etc will be gone. Buy what is cheap now, get your best case, a really really good high wattage PSU, thermal paste, fans etc and hold your money for one- two years for the rest.
>>62294535
>My desktop has been gathering dust for the past 7-8 years. I just use an old laptop for work and music and if I want to play a game I get my 360 or ps2.
I play a lot of XP era games on an XP retro build. Based onn the Ps2 and 360, I do the same but for most it is too late to get a library and they are collectors items now.

62294799
>>62294453
I genuinely see baffling shit like this on /biz/ daily. I'm starting to think being rich makes you retarded.

62294806
>>62294758
Offlinne LANs are the future anon. We both know it.

62294823
>>62294325
The plan is for you to never be able to outright buy your own parts and computer but rather rent the service to play

62294842
>>62294762
There are real bers and zoomer faux nerds. Which one are you? It's the 21 century a man has to have options, money, pussy, drugs, vidya, literature, art, music, film, gardening, hunting, gemology, riding, carpentry, electronics repair., shooting, archery, antqie restoration an collecting, stamp collecting, killing,electronics, programming, driving, motorbikes, falconry, dogs, fitness, martial arts, guitar, piano, harmonica, mandolin, bird spotting, model trains, lego, cooking, writing, academics, casting lea soliders and bulets, sculupture, painting, jewlery making, fine dining, metal work, hiking, military history, tabletop games, photography, war games, chess, thrifting, reading, climbing, tennis, squash, singing, male choir,. Knife sharpening, Making computers, glass blowing, fighting, electronic repair, inciting wars, political philosophy, concert attendence, tracking and killing enemies of your nation, horology, explosives, smallholding, forestry, done racing, learning languages, model aircraft, ham radio, trading crypto stocks and bonds etc etc.

Playing games is just one of those options. They are all fine hobbies, none better that another

item.title: Saturday's almost over, wagie. Tomorrow you'll be too depressed about the coming week to enjoy your time off, so your we… — 21 replies — 33.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Saturday's almost over, wagie. Tomorrow you'll be too depressed about the coming week to enjoy your time off, so your we…

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-05-31 12:48 UTC

OP

Saturday's almost over, wagie. Tomorrow you'll be too depressed about the coming week to enjoy your time off, so your weekend is already spiritually finished. What's that? You didn't come any closer to finding a way out of wagie hell this weekend? OOPS, I guess you'll have to try again next weekend HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA!!!

Early replies (first 3)

62293547
wdym depressed
if im not at work i literally dont know what do do with myself, i just shitpost on /smg/ while waiting for the market to open
I wish I could work 7 days a week, more money I could throw in the market

62293578
>>62293533
I don't even know who these threads are for. Are there /biz/ who work at amazon warehouses? I feel like that would be rare.

62293839
>>62293533
This has to be the real Bezos. Who else could be this consistent?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62293843
>>62293533
you're such an asshole lol

62293898
>>62293533
Well at least I still have both my parents. You don't have your mother who u cried like a bitch at her death after being a dementia vegetable for so long. While she was alive she gazed at you with animal like uncomprehending eyes and u had to gaze back and realize that mom u knew literally doesn't exist

62293907
>>62293533
your rocket ejaculated too early now cleanup that mess Jeffy

62293911
finally we have an answer to him

62293954
>>62293908
Paid for by wagies

62293966
>>62293954
in the end, everything is paid for by wagies, comrade
but in the immediate reckoning, this is on bezos 100%. on his company to be precise, which is a large fraction of his nw
wagies still get their wages even if the rocket goes boom

62293968
>>62293533
at least im not married to some bimbo spic slut

62293969
>>62293533
Saturday to recover
Sunday to prepare
A wagie's weekend vanishes into thin air!

62293976
plz stop mr. bezos

62293981
Every time a bezos thread is posted his start flexing that u have a mother who is alive and doesn't have dementia

62294021
>>62293908
KEK oh nononono bezosissies....

62294023
>>62293533
Was a nice four day week last week wasn't it, wagie. Now you're staring down the barrel of a fresh five

62294025
>>62293908
You will have the incident investigation finalised and on my desk Monday morning sharp, won't you wagie.

62294069
>>62293533
I swear its the real Bezos posting these

62294070
>>62293533
Jokes on you, I'm prepping my resignation letter.

62294254
>>62294069
What else would you do with his wealth? It gives you unlimited power to humiliate wagies.

62294795
>>62293533
It's ok, next week is another week. I will flip so many burgers, I swear. Only 10000 more years of saving 100k a year until I become a billionaire like you, master.

The hopium is real.

item.title: cheap credit line leverage? — 17 replies — 33.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
cheap credit line leverage?

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-05-31 12:55 UTC

OP

Honestly, whats to stop me from doing this
>i have 30k credit line on credit card
>promo offers full credit line conversion to cash debit, at 0% APR for 12 months, 4.5% interest down
>thats like 1500 bucks or so
>tranfser that cash to brokerage
>buy something semi safe thats still obviously going to 2x in a year at minmum (DRAM, AMD, INTC)
>cash out, pay like a third in tax, repay that credit line to 0. ive now doubled my poorfag cash base with 20k
>run it back with a new credit card
>free 0% borrowing against my capital, 0 margin
I know this is really risky, but in the worst case scenario theres like a 33% drop or something that doesnt recover by month 12. then I just liquidate at a loss, use that plus my own capital to repay, which would only be around 10k max. if the market drops more than 33% were all fucked anyway
the one thing you cannot allow to happen is passing the 12 months tho, because then the APR is like 25%

Early replies (first 3)

62294493
Top signal
>were all fucked
From a 33% drop? Oh sweetie

62294497
>>62294493
it seems you missed the part where I said if its more than 33%, it doesnt matter because we are all fucked. thats ok friend, i will reiterate it here.
the point is, you take a seemingly insane risk like this for outsized output

62294513
If you're willing to take on this much risk why not just go balls deep with something that could 10x.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62294515
>>62294497
Maybe people like yourself who want to play with leverage and do stupid shit. You definitely have a lot of company with you considering 90% of the population are cattle soon to be culled

62294522
>>62294515
its really interesting to see your white hot seething just for bringing up a high agency, high risk strategy a person could take. the kind of strategy that actually changes someones situation and proves they are ensouled even if it fail. the opposite of cattle. and it infuriates your insect soul when people devise high agency strategies
i guess you would advocate VOO/VTI passive investing so you can hit 1 million dollars (2025 250k) when youre 70 years old and dying. that or you are a shorter (mentally ill, show positions)

62294636
>>62294482
You want to use leverage when we’re close to the beginning of a bull market, not close to its end like now

62294686
>>62294482
SoFi once gave me a $100k personal loan uncollateralized in 2018 which I used to buy BTC and ETH. Did I regret it? Yes for a couple of years because crypto had a major bear market at that time. But everything eventually bounced back and it's one piece to the puzzle of how I became a millionaire.

62294712
I actually just did this maybe a week or two ago with a similar amount, had 250k in an account then took out a 30k LOC against it @ 4% which is basically nothing. Then I basically put them all into oil producers, expecting them to 2x at the minimum @$80/barrel. Every day the war drags on longer get the that much harder but even at current levels there are a lot of reserves to fill back up and oil production in the middle east has been mcsabotaged to a relatively significant degree. This is just a super basic obvious safe supply/demand play.

62294729
>>62294686
To lend 100k and get 130k back sounds like a better deal.

62294731
>>62294729
They didn't even get that. The loan terms was for 7 or 8 years. I paid it back in 2.

62294744
>>62294686
>uncollateralized
How?

62294747
>>62294744
He's jewish

62294754
>>62294747
Neat lol, me too

62294757
>>62294744
I think it was mostly good timing. Back then SoFi was trying to diversify away from doing only student loan refinancing. All I had to do was a credit check and show proof of my work salary. They weren't strict at all about what the loan could be used for.

62294770
>>62294731
>>62294686
This feels fake as fuck. Annual is 7,70% and over 7 years that would be 7*7 = 49k.
Finance charge says 30k (29,67)
83 payments at 1543 dollars 128000.
Why don't none of the numbers match?
>>62294747
Best explanation

62294805
>>62294770
Are you actually retarded? Look up the standard loan amortization formula.

I'll attach a screenshot from my SoFi app anyway showing my current loans and that paid off personal loan.

62294819
>>62294805
Don't look at the sun too much. What is retarded is having a site that tells you three different costs on the same loan.
>transunion
What the actual fuck?

item.title: Arbitrage — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Arbitrage

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-05-31 13:12 UTC

OP

Good morning sers!

Is there anyone who is able to build arbitrage bot between dexes?

I provide liquidity in Base DLMM DEX and looks like there is big price differences often. aggregators have integrated it but still looks like there is often arb opportunities.

I have no such skills, i tried to build flashloan arb bot but as i'm a smooth brain, it didint work

Thanks

https://docs.sectorone.xyz/info/smart-contracts

Early replies (first 3)

62294332
Which aggregators are routing through those pools?

62294335
>>62294247
Why would anyone who can build a profitable arb bot give it to someone else?

62294407
>>62294335
Out of kindness?

Joke lol, i didint ask bot for myself but someone to run it for better fee printoor

>>62294332
Kyberswap and openocean atleast

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62294410
>>62294247
I am able, but im doing it on Solana and im not gonna touch any ETH-based chain

62294424
Which pools do you need it for? Won’t say no to quick arb profit

62294454
>>62294424
I would prefer it for all pools. when i create new shitter pool, sometimes price difference can be over 40% and arb only happens after i've manually done the trade

62294540
>>62294407
Aight. There is not many users and liquidity is thin, no sense to LP there

62294865
Do you DLMM shitters on base there? Is it as easy as on Solana?

item.title: if Hannibal lost HALF of his 100k men crossing the Alps, how the fuck did i lose half my stack 2020-2025? — 7 replies — 28.0/hr

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-05-31 13:23 UTC

OP

if Hannibal lost HALF of his 100k men crossing the Alps, how the fuck did i lose half my stack 2020-2025?

Early replies (first 3)

62293987
The descendants of those who were lost would have been the buyers pumping your stack today.

62293991
>>62293841
White chuds constantly bring up and think about rome because they have no culture of their own

62294024
>>62293991
Because of shitskin invaders like you

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62294748
>>62294024
Romans were med bvlls that civilized unwashed nigger inlanders, you had no proper roads, no engineering and science to speak of, no water systems, nothing.
"Whites" in the modern sense are absolute niggers.

>My ancestors
WE WUZ ROM- *shush*

62294783
>>62293991
>>62294748
>t.

62294856
>>62294748
What the fuck are you even talking about

62294883
>>62293841
GET THE FUCK OFF THIS BOARD YOU WEEB TRANNY

item.title: Why do workers/poor hate investing? — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why do workers/poor hate investing?

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-05-31 13:24 UTC

OP

Most conversation with friends/family/coworkers I have when it comes to investing long term in Roth IRA, high yield savings, and my ETF Dividend portfolio even with all the money I make working cyber security utilizing FIRE method they get so aggressive about “what if” scenarios thinking I’m day-trading and even when I explain it they don’t get it.

“WHAT IF THE MARKET ALL GOES UNDER AND YOU LOSE EVERYTHING”

“WHAT IF YOU TAKE ALL YOUR MONEY OUT AND SELL ON A LOSS”

“WHAT IF SOMETHING BAD HAPPENS AND YOU HAVE NO MONEY”

Which I retort what happens when you lose your job? When the market goes under and suddenly you’re up for layoffs?

“WELL THAT WILL NEVER HAPPEN BECAUSE IM VALUABLE AND MY SKILLS AND MY TIME AR COMPANY AND-“

Like do these people really think working for someone for the rest of your life is the only way to live? I’d rather live frugally but free, and that really seems to irk people.


What the hell is this psychology? Why do they hate the idea someone doesn’t wanna work for someone for the rest of their life or majority? Do we forget money is to buy our freedom?

Early replies (first 3)

62294872
They are physically, mentally incapable of visualizing what you are trying to explain in terms of long term time horizons, compounding returns, etc. It's not worth bothering with. Like trying to talk to a dog.

62294877
There's a reason poor "people" are poor
The final redpill and the toughest to swallow is that jews are 100% right about the goyim

62294881
>>62294870
You probably come across as obnoxious and irritating and you're clearly in your early 20s and lack the maturity to keep shit to yourself. People don't just randomly get aggressive the way you've described, you're missing the key details and you're probably rubbing people up the wrong way and saying all the wrong this because you lack tact and grace.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62294888
>>62294881
poor people get randomly aggressive for no reason all the time retard, subhuman poors are known to lack impulse control
and what does age has to do with it?
people can be wageslaves until the day they die, doesn't make it a good thing

62294889
>>62294881
Buddy I still got friends who talk nonchalantly about this type of thing and the shit we talk about gets pretty obscene. I don’t talk to anyone I need finesse with unless it’s strictly professional or business.

A lot of assumptions there pal.

item.title: Why hasn't cryptocurrency technology been adopted yet? — 8 replies — 7.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why hasn't cryptocurrency technology been adopted yet?

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-05-31 12:20 UTC

OP

Why hasn't cryptocurrency technology been adopted yet? Allow me to explain.
Cryptocurrencies support decentralized networks, which are used for data computation and storage. These networks are cheaper to use than traditional server infrastructure.
If it's cheaper, why don't corporations use them? One, there are regulations preventing adoption, but CLARITY will change this in the US. Two, corporations have invested trillions into building and maintaining server infrastructure. Liquidation is risky from there point of view because they don't understand decentralized networks. A few brave corporations will lead this great transition, and when they are successful, everyone will follow.
You are in the final window of being early. Don't fuck this up.

Early replies (first 3)

62292772
>>62292765
cope

62292786
>>62292765
How much does 1 Gigabyte on the Bitcoin Blockchain cost?

62292794
>>62292786
>thinks a blockchain is the only data structure for distributed storage

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62292827
too bad clarity won't pass

62292899
CLARITY won't change a thing, the public's appetite for crypto is gone.

62292905
>>62292899
CLARITY is not for the public. It's for corporations.

62294414
>>62292765
>These networks are cheaper to use than traditional server infrastructure.
Beguiled that you would say this with a straight face.

62294730
It's slowly and steadily coming.
t. takes well over a million in cc payments a year.
The "demand" isnt' from pleb retards swiping their cards. It's from business.
We've all moved to ACH/check for all wholesale tx and it's a big swing the last two or three years.
Tough times make people get pissed.
Businesses will get their cheap processing by 2030 I'd guess.

item.title: ETH L1 fees are like 2 cents, Transactions at all time high — 8 replies — 6.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
ETH L1 fees are like 2 cents, Transactions at all time high

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-05-31 12:14 UTC

OP

What in the world is happening ? L1 has literally scaled and fees are dirt cheap . Transactions are at an all time high. Ppl were complaining about ETH fees back in 2021, now they are complaining the price is not rising

62290015
>>62289930
all from hyperliquid

62290381
>>62289930
not enough parasitic rent-seeking of course, this makes investors upset.

62290403
Use case for eth?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62290431
>slow
>ever present mev threat unless you take precautions
>the main cabal is full of insufferable faggots
>All the dapps have UI that was designed by gen y fags
>community of people excited about 6% apy yield farms

i could go on and on. eth is for faggots.

62290521
>>62290403
Its my savings acc.

62290528
>>62290403
Sencond best usecase: its triggering homosexuals

62292588
TVL is also at an all time high

62294720
>>62292588
Cex balance is atl.

item.title: How will the US buying Bitcoin affect Chainlink's price? — 4 replies — 5.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
How will the US buying Bitcoin affect Chainlink's price?

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-05-31 12:41 UTC

OP

How will the US buying Bitcoin affect Chainlink's price?

Early replies (first 3)

62294307
I would check the chart to find the answer to your question. Maybe even the price

62294309
>>62294303
the US has less than 6 months to buy bitcoin, and with republicans blocking bills and budgets for fear of the upcoming elections, it may never happen.

62294419
>>62294309
it will happen
get fucking ready

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62294773
Negatively

item.title: What’s stopping you from buying BTC? — 148 replies — 61.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
What’s stopping you from buying BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 148 • Updated: 2026-05-31 11:00 UTC

OP

What is it? Fear? Lack of conviction? Waiting for lower prices? Or do you genuinely think Bitcoin fails long term?

Curious what’s holding people back at this stage.

Early replies (first 3)

62271721
>>62271712
Btc needs a catalyst. It probably will be yield curve control with a new name. It could also be codifying the SBR into law which is less likely. Or my personal favorite schizo theory, it's discovered that Iran was mining and selling bitcoin to fund it's endeavors and that their supply is finally exhausted.

62271922
>>62271712
Gays in it.

62271933
>>62271712
everybody and their femboy lovers above two-digits IQ are waiting for BTC to dump below at least 40k by July. Why anybody buy now? 4 years cycles never lie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62287162
>>62274011
Work for it anon

62287177
>>62283952
You can already by McDonalds with corn. Use credit card and pay off credit card with corn futures. The problem is your dumbass is bleeding wealth by keeping your net worth in corn (or companies, or anything besides bitcoin) because people are going to make more corn (and ever faster each year due to tech advances) and inflate away your wealth.

62287383
>>62285578
but why remains the hack

62287392
>>62286335
If you're married at don't have money, you deserve a divorce

62287393
>>62271712
It's a gamble and I only bet on sure things. In fact I like to have my profits guaranteed under contract and insured BEFORE I spend a dime of my own money. I always sell BEFORE I buy so I know exactly how much I'm making in advance.

62287464
Most of the retards have been bled dry. BTC only exists now as a system for whales to lead exit pumps and bleed the occasional new entrants.

62287628
>>62287383
Most of the biggest DeFi disasters over the years involved bridges, custodians, or avoidable trust assumptions somewhere in the stack. That's exactly why security architecture gets attention now.

62287630
>>62280441
You're thinking the wrong way

62287632
>>62282426
Well, depends on them

62287634
BTC has just tracked the nasdaq for years until recently and now it just sucks ass no matter what happens.

Hype has peaked. Most of it is owned by boomers now and its not a hedge against anything it just dumps the hardest on bad news until boomers, banks and third worlders get tired of bagholding and it goes sub 20k forever

62287690
>>62287634
So you're saying btc is only a big hell hype?

62288099
>>62287634
nonsense

62288112
>>62273994
So it's not worth it?

62288281
>>62271712
Indifference. I got into the market when the BTC was $3K. but I bought Link instead. Pleased with my decision. I respect what it has done and continues to do with the world but I get excited for smart contracts more, which the BTC doesn't have.

62288653
>>62271712
Because I got caught with my pants down avgcost 95k, and I've been trimming it and using that money as play dump money. So far the 15k I sold from Btc and put into Sandisk have turned in 110k already, and the 10k into gold are at 14k, and it's an actual asset. Never buying that retarded shit again. I still have like 30k and maybe I'll keep that, but chances are I'll probably sell and ride the SpaceX pump and dumo with it. Put in 50k into it, sell 50k when it doubles in price, keep the rest as gains. Did the same shit with Micron, Sandisk and silver, take profits and just keep the rest. This is a hype based market and btc has no hype anymore.

62290061
>>62288112
We'll find out when TBVs actually launch.
Then you can judge the product based on what it does, not on what random people online tell you to think.

62290076
>>62275451
Ok, just buy altcoins

62290078
>>62272002
ahahahahahahahah

62290085
>>62275501
If BTC goes to zero, alts are probably even more cooked.

62290480
>>62287464
If it's such an obvious scam, why are you still here watching it?

62290856
>>62271933
There's truth to this, but I dunno about 40k. Buy "dips" now I say

62290931
>>62272803
>>62272874

The conundrum that niggers on here don't understand is that everyone wants upside volatility, but it's the volatility itself that ultimately creates the ceiling.

It's the cute naive chick that ceases to be so once you fuck her. You can't have your cute and fuck it too.

62291823
>>62290931
You're also another nigger

62292614
>>62287393
So how much are you making?

62292863
>>62271712
the absolute best case scenario for bitcoin would be a 3x. there are other coins currently on the market that can and probably will give me a higher roi.

62292885
>>62271712
Someday a scalable bitcoin will fork away from BTC with enough ideological support to effectively upgrade what everyone will agree to be bitcoin. Then I will buy bitcoin. Until then it's pointless to buy a coin just to get the real bitcoin eventually as a dividend fork. Just wait and buy scalable bitcoin when it exists.

62292989
>>62271712
>saylor holding 5% (more due to lost coins)
>trump stink
>american hegemony stink
>quantum fud
>couldnt even act as a hedge during a war which is its only job
>2028 gary gensler revenge tour
Wasn't the plan this whole time to dump on the institutions? The OGs dumped billions and billions of market clips on them and left forever. That was the entire goal and it was accomplished, genuinely wtf more do you want? The win-con was fulfilled, if you didn't make it then come up with a new one or find another asset. Or you can continue DCAing for the next 30 years I guess

62292997
What are you getting, a 2-4x depending on where the bottom is?

62294119
>>62292863
And what is it? Low cap alts?

62294591
>>62271960
They will say the same thing when it hit 200K Kek. Honestly BTC has a lot of potential most especially since only 1% is in Defi, Imagine how huge it will be with TBV now in play.

item.title: crypto shills destroyed /biz/ — 45 replies — 45.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
crypto shills destroyed /biz/

Open thread • Replies: 45 • Updated: 2026-05-31 12:26 UTC

OP

you stupid shitcunts nobody is going to buy your fucken dumb digital coin that has no fucken use case

You were too late for bitcoin

you got psychologically destroyed by millennial and gen x goobers posting on twitter from their rented condos in miami

And now you're here trying to tiktok dance your way into fucken millions

GTFO

Early replies (first 3)

62293753
>>62293749
don't you have some dying boomer business to take care of lol

62293756
>>62293753
wowee

*does the fortnite dance*

feed my more low i.q. shortform slop sir!

Imagine being a GEN Z male who cannot even focus long enough to fill out a fucken business plan

62293763
>>62293756
imagine being so small brained that you have to work with dirty pipes since its the only way you wont lose everything. its indeed better you leave the crypto to smarter people and focus on the piss and shit

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62293920
>>62293876
No no you're right. Like, it would have been epic if you cashed out on the low 7 figs.

And you do need courage, balls etc to take risk. You are correct.

But any miami cunt who comes at me trying to shill crypto will get crushed under my intellect.

If you're honest bout the game it's all good imo

62293926
>>62293876
also the stealth thing is pretty smart desu. lowers vectors of attack against you. status signalling does have some practical uses but you probably already are smart enough to know when and where to flaunt your status.

62293934
>>62293920
anyone who is spiritually miami deserved to be crushed, that is true.

i'm just some bloke who used tism pattern recognition and intuition to financially help himself and his family. i'm not sorry for any of it and i'd do it all again. i pay my tax like a good goy and don't have to wagie because of this, literally every day i'm free to do as i please. it was worth it desu. take risks in life. doesn't have to be crypto, fuck crypto if you hate it. just bet on yourself in some avenue of life. risk it all.

62293943
>>62293934
for sure, those are great points. And well done!

62294000
>>62293753
What's a non boomer business?

62294194
Chainlink solves multiple real world problems. If you're too dumb to see that, maybe lurk a little longer and read more.
More generally, cryptography, and the financial instruments being developed that are secured by it, strengthens capitalism. It gives more tools to more people and increases the efficiency of the economy. Here's a really simple example for you: tokenized gold that's backed by physical gold. This will enable the whole world to have exposure to hard money. If you can't appreciate that, you should suck your double barrel that's on your nightstand and get the fuck off of my board you decrepit, balding abomination

62294197
>>62293749
Ok newfag

62294203
>>62293749
The wirld is only getting more digital. It only makes sense that digital assets would raise in value. Right now it's a free for all casino. Take it or leave it this is the way it is.

62294208
>>62293826
>i've roundtripped low 7figs
>pokemon cards
The only thing you've roundtripped is the methheads behind the dumpster.

62294210
>>62293749
t top buying baggie.

62294219
>>62294208
>>pokemon cards
i think you're confusing anon here, i never mentioned anything about trading cards.

62294221
>>62294219
My mistake. You are still a homosexual zoomer for investing in shitcoins.

62294231
>>62294221
wow, that was a very rude thing to say.

62294248
>>62293749
>crypto shills destroyed /biz/
/biz/ was created specifically to contain crypto shilling

62294250
>>62294194
>Chainlink solves multiple real world problems.
stopped reading right there, baggie

62294305
>>62294194
>Here's a really simple example for you: tokenized gold that's backed by physical gold. This will enable the whole world to have exposure to hard money
Explain what this means. Seriously, explain it. Tokenized gold that's backed by physical gold. How is that any different than buying 'gold' on an exchange that's just a certificate? They're both digital, but one of them is just stored on a rube-goldberg mechanism instead of a simple database. How does it enable "the whole world" to have exposure to hard money?

You're just saying fucking words.

62294322
>>62294305
>how are tokenized assets different from legacy assets??

t.

62294326
>>62294322
Yes. In a qualitative way, not a quantitative way, explain how they are fundamentally different. Being stored on a slightly different digital substrate doesn't change : you hold a -promise- that you own gold, not actual physical gold.

62294328
>>62294326
Ask your local AI fuckhead.
Nobody here wants to explain the absolute basics of tokenization to you, even the banks have been trying to explain the benefits for over half a decade.

62294331
>>62294328
I want to see you explain it, because you can't, without regurgitating another explanation that will be fille with just as much of a smokescreen.

62294337
>>62294331
listen to yourself

62294340
>>62294337
>a really simple example for you
You're the one who said it. If it's so simple, surely you can bang out a coherent explanation in a minute or two.

You're a tech fetishist. You are mentally bound to this retarded world because you imagine it makes you smart, but it's really just smart in the "grandma thinks he's good with computer" kind of way.

62294365
>>62294340
Take a deep breath you fat boomer, your bp is spiking.

62294367
>>62293749
These sluts must know they are creating wankmaterial. They arent fooling anyone. Skanks. I hate it when they trick my brain into wanting to put my penis inside them. They would be raped 24/7 in the old days.

62294368
>>62294365
I accept your concession.

62294378
>>62293749
That's not even her real body. The app program she's using starts stuttering to keep up as she turns to the side. She's probably built like a soup can.

62294385
>>62293749
use cases are bearish desu

62294446
>>62294385
Probably why memes did so well last cycle. Why i am buying blue chip memes again while they are cooked.

62294649
>>62293756
can you clean my pipe?

62294739
>>62293749
I was at a Calgary Flames hockey game last year and there was a boomer ahead of me who must have drank 7 or 8 tallboys and was just watching shit like this on his phablet.

Sometimes you gotta respect the absolute zero regard boomers have.

item.title: Kek XLM is absolutely ripping and is up multiple places overtaking monero and link while stinkies are at $8.99 LMAO HAHA… — 52 replies — 33.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Kek XLM is absolutely ripping and is up multiple places overtaking monero and link while stinkies are at $8.99 LMAO HAHA…

Open thread • Replies: 52 • Updated: 2026-05-31 11:53 UTC

OP

Kek XLM is absolutely ripping and is up multiple places overtaking monero and link while stinkies are at $8.99

LMAO HAHAHAHAHAHA OH NO NO LINKIES OH NO NO

Early replies (first 3)

62287876
>>62287844
we aren't cuckolds, okay? We just like knowing that Sergey keeps getting richer while Chainlink is stuck at 2020 prices

62288485
kek stinkies

62288499
>>62287844
xlm always does this before the bull..

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62289813
>>62289763
Instance of what?

62289925
>>62287844
I don't care about your (((compliant))) coin

62290228
>>62287844
I started the link cuckold meme. KNEEL

62290247
>>62289745
Chainlink will literally abstract away any gas token

One token all chains (the one and only LINK)

62290559
>>62287844
MOM IT'S DOING IT AGAIN

62290596
>>62289745
kek see this >>62290247

Not only are they going to be buying Link tokens, but they'll be buying Link tokens in order to pay for the gas on the corresponding blockchain. Because the fee in Link takes care of the gas.

62290754
>>62288539
I’ve fucked two guys wives before and filmed it

Idk what possesses dudes to be into this kind of shit but I got paid so

62290965
Xlm will actually hit $1

62291397
>xlm +43%
Damn linkies are fucking cooked.. when's the last time link had a pump like that? 2019??

62291419
>xlm up 100%
Kek linkies

62291508
>Almost in the top #10

Kek stinkies wish they had this kind of performance

62291680
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOm

62291689
>>62287844
Wow XLM sure is running! It must be so much higher than it was a year ago, right?????

62291699
>>62291680
so bullish chainlink

>>62291689
KABOOOOOM!

62291706
>>62291508
>pay tens of millions to Chainlink per year so you can be part of SCALE
>DTCC announces they will be using Chainlink to bridge tokenized assets to XLM using CCIP and exclusively using CRE for orchestration

>LINK -9%
>XLM +80%

62291709
>>62291706
Say it with me

The ... of crypto

62291787
>>62289658
>yet this is how the market reacts.
>No wonder crypto is getting is ass beat by the stock market so badly.

62291789
>>62291787
>market is never wrong

Thats why nothing has ever changed prices in history of human kind

62291989
>>62291787
Stocks are high in the sky and crypto is in the crapper, despite mass institutional adoption finally starting.

Stock market = right
Crypto market = wrong

62292014
>>62291709
THE cuckolds of crypto

62292427
>>62288957
> Big wallets keep accumulating and stacklets keep selling

Can you give a credible source that backs up your claims of whales accumulating and small wallets capitulating

62292431
KABOOOOOOOOOOM

62292813
>>62291989
>>62291789
My nw is 90% in crypto.
The market valuation of chainlink however, is 100% correct.

62293352
Xlm bros…
We got too cocky

62293596

62293668

item.title: *continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're… — 73 replies — 31.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're…

Open thread • Replies: 73 • Updated: 2026-05-31 11:05 UTC

OP

*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path*

All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're close to a buy signal now

prev thread:
>>62261213

Early replies (first 3)

62284885
How do I use this?

62284969
>>62284885
green = buy dips
red = sell rips
yellow = volatility likely, derisk
the site has a realtime FX-based confirmation layer - positive confirms buys and invalidates sells, negative confirms sells and invalidates buys
today FX is solidly green so early buys are defensible
chart gets updated every month with a new 4-week, next will be on Sunday

62284994
imagine buying bitcoin over 70K

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62285967
>>62285940
>CIA
>giving away the free money cheat code to internet autists
doesn't seem very likely anon

62286025
>>62285967
I mean as long as this doesn't go full premium (as a ploy to ensnare gullible /biz/ tards) it seems like a good resource. Some ppl on this board want to stay poor or just FUD for fun. Pathetic behavior honestly.

62286076
>>62286025
It's nowhere close to where I'd feel comfortable charging people money for it. Trade bots/API feeds will probably be subscription, when that's done, but those are more for pro traders
There's also daily updates on the twitter account

62286347
>>62285878
>outputs start to get a little blurry 4-5 weeks out and then just turn into noise, which is why I do the updates every 4 weeks
Why not update weekly? or even daily?

62286447
>>62286347
>Why not update weekly? or even daily?
Basically, because it's a lot easier - there are a lot of inputs into the model that each have to be harvested and processed by me before they get fed into the sim. It runs into the hours each time. I can also cope that the current date's "conditions" are obviously the starting point of the forecast, so having them flattening out a little bit is easier to align
I do weekly updates on the site and the twitter account, but that's 90% just applying current price momentum and FX momentum to the existing physics-based forecast, which I have to get better at. I'm a nerd, not a trader

62286532
>>62284814
>buy may 17
>sell for a loss on May 25

thanks

62286552
>>62286532
>buy may 17
>short may 25
you're welcome

62286639
>>62286447
>because it's a lot easier
Fair enough. Although I'm sure at least some of that data gathering and processing could be automated.
>I'm a nerd, not a trader
So you don't use it all? I get that some people are very risk averse but if you're confident in your technology I don't see why you wouldn't at least dip your toes and make what is essentially free money.

I think I might do a bit of paper trading and I'll let you know how it pans out by the end of June.

62286700
>>62286639
>Fair enough. Although I'm sure at least some of that data gathering and processing could be automated.
A lot of it is! I keep getting tempted to outsource some of it to an LLM but some gut instinct says "you'd be really really dumb to do that," even a local one
>So you don't use it all? I get that some people are very risk averse but if you're confident in your technology I don't see why you wouldn't at least dip your toes and make what is essentially free money.
Oh, I use it, have been trading perps with it for years, getting more profitable as the model evolves. After the addition of the FX layer last spring, I can get away with some truly disgusting leverage. I just meant I don't have a background in trading or technical analysis, so I've been learning all of those skills on the go. It's all related but still a different skillset
>I think I might do a bit of paper trading and I'll let you know how it pans out by the end of June.
Please do, I'll post in here when there's updates but you can always get in touch via Twitter

62287275
>>62284814
I’m just gonna wait for the mega dump to flush the longs down to 60k..

62289468
>>62284814
L, and let me be perfectly clear, FG

62289671
>>62289468
So up from here? Don’t speak zoomzoom

62289838
>>62289671
This is where price should turn up - hybrid FX+physics says Buy because FX is so positive, physics officially flips green tomorrow, it all seems pretty good for bulls over the next 24 hours.

62289954
>>62287275
this. I just go all in bottom of the bear market and do nothing a couple years

62291704
>>62284814
This is no proper backtesting! You must test at least 30 instances!
https://youtu.be/zXoZdnYfIlA?si=MPB49se3bA1H2fnp

62291711
>>62291704
*32 Instances

62291719
>>62291711
>>62291704
?

62291779
>>62291719
How big are the wins and how big are the losses? They have to be in a profitable proportion together with the win rate. 66% win rate with wins 1/3 as big as the losses is not profitable for example.

62291951
>>62284814
broken clock o algo

62292012
what time of day do you make the trades

62292563
if you have a profitable strategy then just make a hyperliquid vault so we can join it and you have 10% profit share

62292730
>>62291779
There's a whole website and twitter account where you can check these things
>>62292012
>a broken clock predicts the future 80% of the time
That's one hell of a broken clock
>>62292012
The demo strategy executes ~2 hours into NY open, when there's enough FX data to confirm or inval the signal
>>62292563
Down the list of things to do. Right now it's a trade bot, which is being built because a mid-tier CEX wants to explore white labelling signals, so the bot needs to be optimized, but then I'll absolutely integrate it into HL

62292891
>>62292730
Thank you, I already have working strategies. I was just advocating doing proper backtesting before applying any strategy.

62292918
>>62292891
>I was just advocating doing proper backtesting before applying any strategy.
Always a good practice - prediction/outcome goes back to December 2024 which is where the hit rate is from. I could backtest to mid-2024 (when the FX layer was added) if I feel like it but I figured 100+ signals over more than a year was good enough

62293541
Spoonfeed me pls OP? Am I supposed to follow your twitter? How do I get the meme lines for myself.

62293559
>>62293541
>How do I get the meme lines for myself.
I'll post them in here, but there's also a live dashboard and a twitter, where a follow/likes would be greatly appreciated
>>62285878

62293571
>>62293559
Thanks for doing the needful sir. I'll be checking this out. Big ups anon.

62293619
>>62292730
Does the trading bot have contingencies in place just in case other trading bots start countertrading your strategy if it ends up being too successful?

62293680
>>62293619
I think if someone else's trading bot can move BTC by a few percent per day then we all have much bigger problems

62294595
>>62284814
Just buy the dip and sell the high simple. The main thing is to hold. I'm not only holding, I also staked some of my BTC recently since we can do that now without bridge involved.

item.title: Question for oldfags about vidya prices: — 53 replies — 21.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Question for oldfags about vidya prices:

Open thread • Replies: 53 • Updated: 2026-05-31 11:01 UTC

OP

i looked into the price of SNES games as well as master system back in the days:

-average snes game sold for 50$ in the early 9às, inflation adjusted that's 120$ today
Some sold higher than that : donkey kong came for 70$, so 150$ today

-30 to 60$ for sega master system, but it was the late 80s so like 130$ today.

Why are gooners crying about the price of their vidyas? Things used to be way more expensive for a 2 d platformer that would give you max 100 hours of play if not half. Also today's collector price for most of those games is still LOWER than what people paid for when it came out brand new back in the days, so still no profit after holding for 30+ years kek

Early replies (first 3)

62283131
>>62283119
There was a time not so long ago when you could hardly give that shit away. Nobody wanted to play old 2D games when there was amazing new stuff coming out that you couldn't have even imagined a few years prior.

62283133
>>62283119
Oldfag here, they’re crying because all these games were $1 at your local flea market in 2005, AND THEY DIDN’T EVEN WANT TO BUY. It’s even worse than missing bitcoin because it was right under their nose, they saw, they knew, and they shrugged it off.

62283135
>>62283131
snes games were cheap like 10 + years ago. Master system is still very cheap, but nobody gives a shit. I guess rn the money is in xbox / game cube games

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62286565
>>62283119
They're bitching just to bitch. There's also like 1000 ways to emulate those games and play them like the originals or better, so really they're retardedly nostalgic to have THE cartridge and then mad a bunch of other people are too

62286669
>>62283133
Nobody is talking about used video games. They're crying because modern consoles are approaching a thousand bucks and moden games are heading towards 100. But anon correctly noted that back in the day, it was $50-60 for a new NES cartridge or a new computer game on three 360kb diskettes, and at the time minimum wage was something like $4 an hour. Adjusted for inflation, a game like Super Mario that you could beat in an hour, or Kings Quest that lasts you a couple of hours max, cost $161.

62287647
>>62283131
>There was a time not so long ago when you could hardly give that shit away. Nobody wanted to play old 2D games when there was amazing new stuff coming out that you couldn't have even imagined a few years prior.
This
I bought a movie rental stores stock for peanuts when they were going broke in 2012, walked in and offered 2000$ cash for the lot (about 5000 DVDs and blu rays and 2500 PS1, PS2 and 360 games). It was a niche movie buff DVD and game store. Have a pretty complete movie and game library and enjoy it a lot to this day. I am randy marsh.

62287668
>>62286669
>Nobody is talking about used video games.
OP was literally talking about "today's collector price for most of those games" but okay

62287673
>>62287668
both can come into discussion, but the idea was that game used to be even more expensive than they are today yet people bitch about their free world 10k hours lifetime game selling for half what a super nintendo game used to sell

62287727
>>62283119
I don't know what a SNES is (Nintendo consoles are for faggots), but I used to buy pirated PS1/PS2 games for $5 from some shady dude back in the day. Even back then, only retards would pay $50 for a video game. I see times haven't changed...

62288182
>>62287668
Yes.
>today's colllector price is still LOWER than what people paid for when it came out brand new
He's still talking about how prices today are still lower when accounting for inflation, even for used collector games, compared to the price for games back in the day.
And he's right. A nintendo entertainment system with one game (Mario/Duck Hunt) adjusted for inflation cost $450.
A New 386 16 with 1mb of ram and a 20mb hard drive with 16 color graphics and midi sound card cost $8500, adjusted for inflation that's $25,000.
People bitching about prices for games these days really have no idea. Their idea of what things cost back in the day is skewed by a combination television where the latest technology was placed "in the common home" as a deliberate advertisement and a long run of (relatively) dramatically discounted prices from the late 90s until the late 10s.
Even today their expectations are ridiculous: "It shouldn't cost as much as when you had physical media because you can just download it!", like servers and connections that can provide fast 85gb video game downloads to millions of people around the world are not expensive themselves.

62288229
>>62283119
People's real wages were also higher.

62288318
>>62283119
I played that game on that console when I was a kid.

62288477
>>62287673
>>62288182
This is true, video games are cheaper in real terms and even in nominal terms today. Even the "expensive" Switch games are not pricier than Street Fighter II in 1993. But the essential things of life are much more expensive (rent, food), which makes video games comparatively just as hard (or harder?) to afford. Hence the complaining.

The crybabies will also complain when a game is free though:
>waaaah I don’t get enough pulls to get my waifu in Genshin Impact, even though I spent $0 in five years of playing daily

62288623
>>62288477
>>62288318
>>62288182
i had a friend at school who had a dedicated playroom room with every fucking super nes game as well as mega drive. Spoiled brat, parents probably were LOADED. That made me diamond at the time

62288647
>>62283119
vidya felt way more expensive and special. buying a new vidya was an important life decision for many, you had to study that shit, read game magazine articles about vidya to weigh which to invest in, you had to save up money, potentially sell other vidya to frens for a bit of cash (like actual paper bills or even actual metal coins). back then when a neighbor kid got a new vidya it was an event and kids would gather to check it out or ask to borrow it someday. i invested all my money in vidya and bought a new one per month. don't get me started on buying a new console, that felt similar to buying a new fridge, something you needed to buy for basic survival because the old one was just not doing it anymore.

62288698
>>62288623
>>62288647
Kek, back then you knew exactly how many games you had, and could recite the list of all your games like it’s a poem.

Each game was considered a rather big purchase and the games felt more "precious" in general. Also true with the PS1-era games.

62288801
>>62283119
muh inflation is a cope shit tier non-argument.
snes and mega drive games were also printed on very expensive cartridges and game makers also had to pay a lot to retail.
now most sales are digital, so there is literally zero actual scarcity or logistics involved and the market is way bigger than it ever was.

basically kys

62289331
>>62288229
>People's real wages were also higher.
No, they really weren't. The people making this claim base it on the price of Gold in 1970 and extrapolate it from there. (or they compare themselves to characters on 70s and 80s television) The price of Gold was artificially suppressed for 30 years by Bretton Woods. The median salary in 1987 was $30,000 a year. If you made above 60,000 you were upper middle class. Pic related was a middle class home and cost 120k new. People also like to pretend that there weren't oil shocks and 20% inflation in the 70s and 80s wiping out that majority of boomers' savings, or that a mortgage for such a home didn't come with a 16% interest rate.

62289351
>>62288801
>there is literally zero actual scarcity or logistics involved
Servers and high speed connections are not zero logistics.
You don't even put two and two together. In the heady days of the wild west internet of the early 2000s, bandwidth was cheap, but it was also slow. People don't realize that because they demanded HD (and later 4K) streaming that infrastructure costs have skyrocketed so much that only the richest corporations can afford to provide that kind of service. You wonder why there's so much consolidation of media, why the major corporations have bought up so many smaller studios, why social media and video streaming is reduced to a handful of winners, it's because the bandwidth necessary to transmit the large amounts of data you're demanding is huge, and Bandwidth ain't actually cheap at all.

62289407
>>62283119
games used to actually be good, and they provided you with hundreds of hours of entertainment. not the dog shit coming out today that you beat in 8 hrs and then never play again

62291136
>>62283119

62291154
>muh inflation
>>62289351
The developer would have gotten 7 dollars off of a 70 dollar 4MB SNES cartridge. Seven.
Today they get around 20. Adjust that for inflation and they've beaten inflation. About 15 bucks if you take cpi is what the cut in the snes era was worth.

Everything about digital distribution is cheaper than sticking roms into plastic boxes and shipping them internationally for them to sit in warehouses and later retail stores. And it could be even cheaper Steam's monopoly keeps things artificially high.

62291182
>>62291154

I've been buying off GOG lately, unfortunately they don't have everything.

And now things are so warped people think gamecube is the best Nintendo console.

62292811

62294347
>>62283119
My brother sold my Earthbound game that I bought when it came out for like $600 a few years back. The whole thing was a sting operation and the buyer was really a cop who then checked it to see if it was real. I think my brother got to keep the $600 and the cops took the real game.

62294592
>>62283119

Well keep in mind there was only two consoles worth giving a fuck about.. most of us or at least the people i knew we all rented games from the video shop.. we were born into our parents going to the video shops and renting us videos to watch, so naturally that extended to gaming. For a couple dollars i played donkey kong for about 3 days when it came out. Owning games were like getting only 1-2 games per year on birthdays and xmas , we also shared a lot of our games with friends. There wasnt many good games to choose from so if you had mario and something else you were golden for the long haul. We got our main gaming fix from arcade centers, they were big back in those days ~ sega nintendo were still very inferior to what arcades provided, they were what it seemed generations behind of their time up until the ps1 came out that started the change, then cheaper computers and faster internet = death of arcades + video shops

tldr: we did more with less
